observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant and General Services Officer undertake the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the City following the guidelines and procedures prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 in order to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and/or the City Administrator to:
Closely coordinate with the COA – ICFAO regarding compliance with the requirements in the Audit Observation Memoranda (AOMs) and Notices of Suspension (NS) issued by said office so that a credit notice or advice can be correspondingly issued;
Seek advice from the COA – ICFAO on alternative actions to be done for Intelligence/Confidential accounts whose liquidation documents could not be located anymore and considering that some responsible persons in the AOMs issued were no longer connected with the City; and
File request for authority to write off dormant accounts of the former City Mayor considering that he is already deceased as well as part of his accounts have been outstanding for  10 years and compliance with the requirements in the NS cannot be undertaken as required, following the procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Direct the City Treasurer/Accountant to institute the withholding of salary from Ms. Armen Tabligan while collectability of the outstanding amount of her cash advance is still viable considering that she is still in the active service, pursuant to Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
Require the City Accountant to prepare the necessary documents for the request for write-off of long outstanding dormant cash advances, following the provisions under COA Circular                    No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the City Agriculturist:
Conduct inventory of other breeding stocks not yet physically counted and submit inventory reports to support the breeding stocks and other biological assets as of December 31, 2021; and
Provide the prevailing market price of each item of breeding stocks as basis of the City Accountant in taking up adjustments in the books, pursuant to Section 4.5 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated                      September 30, 2016 and PPSAS 27 in order to reflect a correct and reliable balance of Breeding Stocks account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Take up accrued interest payable for accrual of interest on loans-domestic pursuant to Paragraph 27 of PPSAS I and COA Circular     No. 2015-009; and
Reclassify loan amortizations due for more than twelve months from balance sheet date as non-current liabilities in order to reflect a fair presentation of said account in the Statement of Financial Position as at year-end pursuant to Paragraph 80 of PPSAS I.

We recommended that the City Mayor terminate the Time Deposit account number 2571-0661-92 with the LBP, Victorias branch, after its maturity and transfer the same to the existing current/savings account of the City under TF so that the fund is readily available for the implementation of specific programs, projects and activities for which the trust was created or the funds received in consonance with Sections 3 and 4 of PD No. 1445 and Section 21 of COA Circular No. 92-383 dated July 3, 1992.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
The City Accountant to conduct thorough review of the financial statements under the TF books and determine the reasons of the cash overdraft other than those already identified during the audit and ensure the existence of funds held in trust before certifying in the DV or for any disbursements related thereto; and
The City officials certifying the disbursements ascertain that TF disbursements or obligations are in accordance with the trust agreement or approved budget and used only for purposes for which these are intended or created pursuant to Section 305, Item E, of the Local Government  Code  of 1991, Section 2 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 100 and 101 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Direct the City Budget Officer, City Treasurer and City Accountant to charge the loan amortizations of the 2 ambulances under the 20% DF, vehicles assigned under the DRRMO against the DRRM funds and all other remaining motor vehicles against the unrestricted funds under the General Fund; and
Instruct the City Accountant, City Budget Officer and City Planning and Development Coordinator to ascertain that only transactions/expenses related to programs/projects/activities embodied in DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 should be charged against the 20% DF and ensure to adhere to the guidelines of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2021-004 dated January 18, 2021.

We recommended that the City Accountant review the balances pertaining to fund transfers received by the City from the Province of Negros Occidental and recommend to the City Mayor to request authority from the PNO to transfer the unexpended balance to the General Fund for the possible reprogramming of the fund for other projects.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Record all DRRMF-related cash donations under the Trust       Liabilities – DRRM Fund account in consonance with Item IV.B.3 of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; and
Adhere to the accounting and reporting guidelines on the utilization of cash and in-kind donations from local and foreign sources as prescribed in said COA Circular order to achieve transparency of and accountability in the use of disaster relief aid/donations.

We recommended that Management submit specific law, other statutory authority, or rules and regulations of competent authority expressly authorizing such payment of additional benefit to its officials and employees pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-003 dated January 30, 2013. Otherwise, such payment is devoid of legal basis.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the concerned personnel to submit the lacking documents, relative to the payment of COVID-19 AHDP and SRA, such as the Authority or Office Order and DTR/Attendance Records or certification regarding the number of days that the frontline workers physically reported for work from                  September 15, 2020 to December 19, 2020, pursuant to DBM-DOH JC Nos. 1 and 2 dated November 25, 2020.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Direct the General Services Office to conduct a complete physical inventory count of all properties, plant and equipment and report the actual results in the RPCPPE, pursuant to the requirement of Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
Require the Accounting and General Services offices to reconcile the balances of PPEs based on the result of actual physical count with the PPE balances per PPE subsidiary ledgers (SL).  Moreover, conduct immediate investigation on the unreconciled and unidentified PPE accounts recorded in lumped amounts in order to establish the propriety of all PPE balances.

We recommended that the City Mayor and/or the City Accountant:
Direct the personnel responsible to comply with the requirements in the Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) and Notice of Suspension (NS) issued by COA – ICFAO so that the latter can correspondingly issue a credit advice; and
Inquire from COA-ICFAO whether the City could possibly file a request for write off of dormant unliquidated cash advances of former Mayor Severo A. Palanca considering that he was already deceased, his accounts have been outstanding for almost 10 years and could not anymore comply with the requirements in the NS.

We recommended that the City Treasurer or the City Accountant:
Issue a third and final demand relative to the unliquidated cash advances to officers and employees and special disbursing officer totaling P698,288.85 as of December 31, 2020.
Institute the withholding of salary from Ms. Armen Tabligan if the same remains to be unsettled after the issuance of third and final demand, pursuant to the second paragraph of Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 while collectability of the amount is still viable; and
Request for the write off of long outstanding dormant advances aging 10 years and over found to be uncollectible, following the provisions under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer and/or the City Accountant:
Immediately reconcile the accounts of the AO per cashbook versus the SL and adjust the discrepancies at once. Thereafter, conduct regular reconciliation of balances of the accounts of the AOs pursuant to Section 181 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume I to avoid the accumulation of reconciling items;
Ensure that no additional cash advance is granted to the Accountable Officer unless the previous cash advance is first settled in consonance with the requirements of Item 4.1.2. of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
Submit the Report of Disbursement together with all the vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents to the Auditor within 10 days after receipt of the report and supporting documents from the AO as required under Items 5.2 and 5.3. of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the City Accountant re-compute the amortization of the Intangible Assets based on the correct useful life of the assets and take up adjustment for prior years’ amortization as Prior Years’ Adjustment to be closed to the Government Equity account while the current years’ amortization be recorded as expense.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Submit all the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers and supporting documents from January to December 2020 to the Office of the Auditor; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant and General Services Office
   conduct a complete reconciliation of their  records  by  identifying  the
   discrepancies, determine the causes and prepare necessary adjustments, if
   any, pursuant to Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.


        Further,  we  recommended  that  the  City  Accountant   reclassify
   unserviceable inventories included in the PPE accounts  into  its  proper
   account  and  accordingly,  make  adjustment  in  the  books   once   the
   unserviceable inventory items are disposed of.

We recommended that the City Mayor and/or the City Accountant:

        a. Strictly require officials and employees to liquidate their cash
           advances within the prescribed periods pursuant to  5.1  of  COA
           Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.  In  case  of  non-
           compliance thereof, send demand letters to these  officials  and
           employees to  settle  the  overdue  amounts  within  a  specific
           period. Otherwise, effect the withholding of their salaries;


        b. Closely  coordinate  with  COA-ICFAU  regarding  the  status  of
           previously submitted liquidation documents  or  charges  against
           previous years’ confidential fund.  Moreover,  ensure  immediate
           compliance  to  the  requirements  in  the   Audit   Observation
           Memorandum (AOM) issued by COA-ICFAU so that said  office  could
           correspondingly issue a credit advice or credit notice;

        c. Review the  Unreconciled  Other  Advances  totaling  P461,974.43
           included  in  the  Report  on  Unliquidated  Cash  Advances  and
           determine the specifics of these  transactions  to  include  the
           name of the persons granted with cash advances, the date of  the
           grant,  the  amount  of  cash  advance  and  the  due  date   of
           liquidation;


        d. Review the accounts of employees who  are  no  longer  connected
           with the City and  determine  whether  their  unliquidated  cash
           advances can be deducted from their claims/collectibles from the
           City, if there is any; and

        e.  File  request  for  authority  to  write-off  dormant  or  long
           outstanding unliquidated cash advances,  if  necessary,  to  the
           Office of the Auditor pursuant  to  COA  Circular  No.  2016-005
           dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the City fast track the implementation  of  all
   remaining BuB projects funded from the national  government  agencies  to
   ensure that the desired  benefits/services  could  be  delivered  to  the
   City’s constituents on time and could  address  the  needs  of  the  poor
   sectors of the City.

        We also recommended that the Project Monitoring Committee of  Local
   Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) or designated focal person  monitor
   the implementation of all BuB projects pursuant to the  guidelines  under
       DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC   Joint   Memorandum   Circular   No.   7    dated
   November 3, 2015.

        Likewise, for fully implemented projects with unutilized  balances,
   the Accounting Office must determine whether or not these funds are still
   needed, otherwise, make the necessary return or refund of these funds  to
   the source agencies.

We recommended that the City Treasurer/Market Supervisor:


        a.       Strictly monitor that delinquent tenants  adhere  strictly
           to the Compromise Agreement and prepare a report for  those  who
           are not compliant to the agreement. As  to  those  tenants  with
           status accounts, ensure strict  enforcement  of  the  terms  and
           conditions embodied in the Contract of Lease; and


        b.        Enforce  collection  of  outstanding  electricity   bills
           through the issuance of  demand  letters.  On  the  other  hand,
           ascertain that all tenants  are  billed  based  on  their  water
           consumption and not only to preferred few occupants to  increase
           the income and decrease unnecessary expenses at VCC.


        We also recommended that the VCC  management  immediately  evaluate
   the cases  of  vacant  or  unoccupied  stalls  by  weighing  the  options
   available to the tenants who were affected of closures. Further, in cases
   of other block/stalls that have not been rented for a  longer  period  of
   time, the VCC management must formulate plans  and  adopt  strategies  to
   address this problem with the end view of generating more  income,  which
   can help augment its operations.

We  recommended  that  the  City  of  Victorias  ensure  that   the
   Memorandum of Agreement to NGO/PO must contain all the  applicable  terms
   of reference enumerated under Item 4.5.3 and Item 4.5.8 of  COA  Circular
      No. 2007-001 and  the  project  name  clearly  indicated  in  the  MOA
   pursuant to Item 4.3 of same Circular.




        We further recommended that the  City  Accounting  Office  strictly
   evaluate release of Fund Transfer to NGOs/POs and no  disbursement  shall
   be certified unless supported with the prescribed Memorandum of Agreement
   as required under 4.5.3 a) to m), COA Circular No. 2007-001.

We recommended that the City  Mayor  ensure  that  the  VCDRRMO  is
   manned by mandatory permanent positions as mandated under Section  12  of
   R.A. No. 10121 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1  of  the  NDRRMC,
   DILG, DBM and CSC. Additionally, he should appoint a head of  the  Office
   based on the qualification  standards  appropriate  to  the  position  as
   provided under 6.2 of said JMC.

We recommended that the VCDRRMC:

        a) Formulate a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP using  as  basis
           the recently  approved  National  Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and
           Management Plan (2011-2028) to be accustomed to the City’s needs
           for   prevention   and   mitigation,   preparedness,   response,
           rehabilitation and recovery pursuant to NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC
                       No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and


        b) Review and update annually the VCDRRMAIP to  include  PPAs  that
           will address the adverse impacts of disaster  that  might  occur
           anytime  in  the  community  and  to  build  disaster  resilient
           communities.

We recommended that the City Accountant:

        a.       Record all fund transfers to barangays under Due from LGUs
           account instead of Subsidy to LGUs for easy  monitoring  of  all
           fund transfers to barangays including their liquidations; and


        b.        Certify  that  previous  funds   transferred   has   been
           liquidated and accounted for in the  books  in  compliance  with
           Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012  and
           COA Circular No. 2016-002 dated May 31, 2016.

We recommended that the City Treasurer and the City Accountant:

        a. Conduct immediate review on the noted  discrepancy  and  prepare
           appropriate adjustments promptly to reflect correct and reliable
            Cash – Local Treasury balance of all funds; and


        b.       Reconcile  at  least  quarterly  the  Cash-Local  Treasury
           account pursuant to Section 181 of GAAM, Volume I  to  determine
           unrecorded transactions, errors and corrections for  appropriate
           adjustment in their records.

We recommended that:

        a. The  City  Treasurer  closely  monitor  the  activities  of  all
           Accountable Officers especially on the proper maintenance of the
           cashbook by posting  transactions  daily,  footing  and  closing
           every end of the month;


        b.       The Accountable Officer immediately review the transaction
           entries in her cashbooks, ensure the proper footing and  closing
           of the books and certification therein every end of the month to
           ascertain the correctness and completeness of  the  transactions
           recorded; and

         c.      The Accountable Officer and the City Accountant  reconcile
           their books of accounts at least quarterly pursuant  to  Section
           181(c) of GAAM, Volume I.

We recommended that the City of Victorias submit  legal  basis  for
   the grant of additional bonus  in  December  2018  to  its  City  elected
   officials and employees. Henceforth, ensure that benefits  or  incentives
   granted to City officials and employees are expressly  provided  by  law,
   and other statutory authority and the rules and  regulations  promulgated
   by competent authority  pursuant  to  COA  Circular  No.  2013-003  dated
   January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the City Accountant, City  Budget  Officer  and
   City   Planning   and   Development   Coordinator   ensure   that    only
   transactions/expenses related to programs/projects/activities embodied in
   DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular  No.  2017-1  shall  be  charged  against
   the 20% Development Fund in order to  achieve  or  attain  the  desirable
   socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

   the following necessary documents to support that the Local School  Board
   has complied with the guidelines in the planning and budgeting of the SEF
   pursuant to Item 5.0 of Joint Circular No. 01, series 2017 of DEPED,  DBM

Recommendation:

   grant of Employee Incentives Benefits to the City employees for CY 2017
   and onwards. Henceforth, he must ensure that benefits or incentives
   granted to City officials and employees are expressly provided by law,
   and other statutory authority and the rules and regulations promulgated
   by competent authority pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-003 dated
   January 30, 2013.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Mayor and the Accountant:

        a. Require the Accountable Officers to liquidate their  outstanding
           cash advances, through submission of  the  necessary  supporting
           documents and refund the unexpended balance, if any.


        b.       Immediately send demand letters to officials and employees
           who have long outstanding cash advances to  settle  the  overdue
           amounts  within  a  specific  period.  Otherwise,   effect   the
           withholding of their salaries pursuant to 5.1.3 of COA  Circular
           No. 97-002 until such time that  the  cash  advances  are  fully
           settled.


        c.       Strictly require  officials  and  employees  to  liquidate
           their cash advances within the prescribed  periods  pursuant  to
           5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.


        d.       Immediately send  a  follow  up  letter  to  COA–ICFAU  to
           ascertain  the  status  of  previously   submitted   liquidation
           documents for charges against previous years’ confidential  fund
           (CY 2014 and below) and request issuance of  credit  advice  and
           credit notice.


        e.       File request for authority to write-off dormant or long
           outstanding unliquidated cash advances, if necessary, to the
           Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005
           dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Accountant:

        a. Determine the purpose of the funds received from DBM –  Regional
           Office VI, whether it is intended for PDAF or not.

        b.       Refrain from making charges  against  the  remaining  PDAF
           funds and prepare immediately a list of unexpended PDAF balances
           which will serve as the basis in the preparation of disbursement
           voucher by the City Treasurer for the refund of  the  amount  to
           the Bureau of Treasury in  compliance  with  the  Supreme  Court
           ruling issued on November 19, 2013.
        c.       Prepare a soon as the amount  is  returned  the  necessary
           adjustment to book up  the  reversion  of  the  unutilized  PDAF
           funds.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Government specifically the GAD  Focal
   Point System shall submit the following:


        a) Result  of  Gender  Analysis  using  sex-disaggregated  data  or
           statistics for Victorias City Scholarship Program.


        b) CY 2017 GAD Plan and Budget approved by the City Mayor  and  the
           Regional DILG.


        c) CY 2017 Accomplishment Report  (AR)  with  identified  or  well-
           defined gender issues that were addressed by the City Government
           intervention thru the Scholarship Program.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Accountant:

        a.       Strictly comply with the documentary requirements for  the
           transfer of  funds  to  barangay  as  implementing  agencies  of
           government program and projects  pursuant  to  Section  4(6)  of
           Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular
           No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.


        b.       Record fund transfers to barangays  under  Due  from  LGUs
           account instead of Subsidy to Local Government  Units  for  easy
           monitoring  of  all  fund  transfers  to  barangays  and   their
           liquidations.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that:

        a.       The City Accountant ensure the completeness  and  validity
           of required documents enumerated under 2.1 of COA  Circular  No.
           2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before certifying the disbursements
           for the transfer of fund to NGO.

        b.       The City Mayor refrain from approving transfer of fund  to
           NGO without complete supporting documents enumerated  under  2.1
           of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.


        c.       The signing Officials  of  the  City  must  ascertain  that
           the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA), which is a mandatory  requirement
           for the release of financial assistance to NGO shall embody  all
           the terms of reference enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Government appropriate  the  budgetary
   requirement equivalent to 1% of the IRA for LCPC programs,  projects  and
   activities in compliance with R.A.  No.  9344,  otherwise  known  as  the
   Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the City SWMB to  convene
   as soon as possible to update, finalize the 10-Year SWM Plan  and  submit
   the lacking requirements to hasten  the  approval  of  the  plan  by  the
   National Solid Waste Management Commission.

        In updating the SWMB plan, the Board must anticipate  expansion  of
   solid waste management services, provision of a more cost-effective waste
   collection and transport and establishment of cost effective solid  waste
   disposal facility.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that:


        a. The LDRRM Council may propose for the revision of the LDRRM Plan
           and introduce new activities and  projects  that  would  reduce,
           avoid, respond and manage the adverse impacts  of  disasters  in
           order to achieve the country’s overall goal of “Safer,  adaptive
           and   disaster   resilient   communities   towards   sustainable
           development.”


        b. The City Accountant to prepare and certify the Report on Sources
           and Utilization of DRRMF using the format  in  Annex  B  of  COA
           Circular No. 2012-002 and the LDRRM Officer to submit  the  said
           report to COA on or before the 15th day after the  end  of  each
           month.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City  Accountant  adhere  strictly  on  the
   submission  of  the  financial  statements   and   supporting   schedules
   especially year-end FS and must exert extra effort to submit the same  on
   or before the deadline pursuant to COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated  March
   2, 2010 and COA Circular 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the City  Budget  Office,
   CPDO, City Engineer’s Office  and  all  other  offices  involved  in  the
   planning and execution to have a regular monitoring and assessment of the
   rate of progress in project implementation to achieve the  desired  level
   of efficiency so that the benefits  and  services  which  go  with  these
   projects could be delivered to the constituents on time and without delay
    and maximize utilization of the 20% DF so it could benefit the  intended
   beneficiaries.

Recommendation:

        We  recommended  that  the  Local  Disaster  Risk   Reduction   and
   Management Office maximize the utilization of the prior years’ LDRRMF  in
   the Special Trust Fund including the continuing appropriations under  the
   General Fund so it could serve the purpose of equipping the City  against
   the adverse impacts of hazards and related disasters.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City fast track the implementation  of  all
   BUB projects funded from the national government agencies to ensure  that
   the  desired  benefits/services  could  be  delivered   to   the   City’s
   constituents on time and could address the needs of the poor  sectors  of
   the City.

        We also recommended that the Project Monitoring Committee of  Local
   Poverty Reduction Action  Team  (LPRAT)  or  designated  focal  person
   monitor the implementation of all BuB projects pursuant to the  guidelines
     DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7  dated      November
   3, 2015

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Government:


        1.        Enforce  strict  implementation  of  the  provisions   of
           Sections 21 and 22 of RA 9003 on the  mandatory  segregation  of
           solid wastes and carry out the following:


           a.    Strengthen Information and Education  Campaign  activities
              like continuous information dissemination relative  to  solid
              waste management.


           b.    Establish close coordination with  barangay  officials  to
              ensure that  efforts  to  improve  the  SWM  will  reach  the
              constituents at the barangay level.


           c.    Develop a strategic scheme, like providing incentives, for
              the purpose of encouraging  constituents  or  enterprises  to
              develop or undertake responsible and  effective  solid  waste
              management.


           d.    Impose  fines  and  penalties  for  any  offense  made  or
              violation of Section 48 of RA 9003.

        2.       Purchase additional dump trucks to augment the hauling  of
           solid wastes of  barangays.  Likewise,  provide  collection  and
           transport schedules and require the use of separate  trucks  for
           specific types of wastes as required by Section 24 of RA 9003.


        3.       Ensure the establishment or  maintenance  of  MRF  in  all
           barangays pursuant to Section 32 of RA 9003.


        4.       Implement the closure of  the  open  dump,  construct  and
           operate a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site  for  solid
           wastes  to  ensure  protection  of   public   health   and   the
           environment.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Accountant and  the  General  Services
   Officer:

        a. Conduct immediate reconciliation of their  records  as  provided
           under Section  114  of  the  MNGAS  for  LGUs  to  identify  the
           discrepancies and prepare appropriate adjustments if needed.


        b. Investigate and require explanations from personnel responsible
           and liable of any PPE not presented or not found during actual
           inventory, particularly for material discrepancies noted between
           the Accounting and GSO records pursuant to Section 491-B, Volume
           I of the GAAM.


   Management Comment:


        Recommendation is well taken. Close coordination between staff in-
   charge of GSO and Accounting for the reconciliation of differences.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that concerned City officials submit immediately the
   lacking documents as enumerated above as mandated by Section  13.1  of  COA
   Circular No. 2012-001 and Item B of Article 35 of Rule VI of the  IRR  of
   RA 7160 to facilitate audit and evaluation of the contract by  the  Audit
   Team and for further actions to be undertaken.

   Management Comment:


        Recommendation is well taken.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City officials assign focal persons in the
   ESA and HOMA programs of the DSWD and NHA, respectively, and submit
   promptly the required documents pursuant to DSWD Memorandum Circular No.
   24, Series of 2014 for ESA and HOMA beneficiaries as proof of the
   propriety of the payment.


   Management Comment:


        Recommendation is well taken.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Mayor require the BAC to prepare the
   PMR to be approved by the HOPE and submit the same to the GPPB in printed
   and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each
   semester pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184.


   Management Comment:


        Recommendation is well taken.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC/Inspectorate
   Committee to furnish the Audit Team copies of perfected/issued contracts
   and POs/JOs and its supporting documents, as well as inspection and
   acceptance reports within the prescribed period mandated by COA Circular
   Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.


   Management Comment:


        Recommendation is well taken.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the BAC adheres strictly on the mandatory
   requirements provided under Sections 13, 21 and 34 of the 2016 RIRR of RA
   9184 and COA Circular No. 2012 -001 regarding the procurement of goods
   and infrastructure projects to ascertain the validity and authenticity of
   the BAC proceedings.


   Management Comment:


        Recommendation is well taken.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the BAC require the supplier/contractor to post
   warranty security for one year as prescribed under RA 9184 to protect the
   interest of the government against defects in the procurement of goods
   and infrastructure projects.



   Management Comment:


        Retention Fee is not released without the Supplier complying with
   the Warranty Security. Copies of warranty security for identified
   transactions shall be submitted to COA as soon as possible.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the agency comply with the requirements of Item
   6.1.8.4  Joint  Circular  No.  2015-01  dated  January  8,  2015  on  the
   submission of cash advance vouchers  on  confidential  expenses  together
   with the supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.


   Management Comment:


        Recommendation is well taken.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the City SWMB to  convene
   as soon as possible to finalize the 10-year City SWM Plan  including  its
   current plan for approval by the National Solid Waste Management Board.

        Additionally, the Board must regularly meet as  mandated  under  RA
   No. 9003 and the City Ordinance No. 08-2013 so  they  could  monitor  the
   implementation of programs, projects and activities once the  City’s  SWM
   Plan is approved.


   Management Comment:


        The City has already started discussion for the revival of the City
   Solid Waste Management Board. Among the issues tackled is the hiring of a
   competent professional to prepare and manage the implementation of the 10-
   year City Solid Waste Management Plan.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Government:

        a. Implement strictly the provisions of RA 9003 and  intensify  the
           “no segregation, no collection policy” among residents of  every
           barangay.  Further, direct all barangay and  barangay  officials
           to coordinate closely with the SWMB on this policy.



        b. Implement the closure of the open dump site  as  mandated  under
           Section 41 of RA 9003 and  the  possibility  of  converting  the
           present dump site into a sanitary landfill and/or  converting  a
           new technological  approach  to  solid  waste  management  where
           wastes are better managed, controlled and contained.


        c. Purchase additional dump trucks to  haul  solid  wastes  of  all
           barangays and provide specific  collection  schedules  for  each
           type of waste.


        d. Direct the SWMB  to  ensure  that  all  barangays  in  the  City
           establish Material Recovery Facilities pursuant to Section 32 of
           RA 9003.

        e. Exercise strong political will to  implement  the  environmental
           laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated by
           the Act.


   Management Comment:


        The City has re-started it program on Waste Segregation on May  10,
   2017.  “No segregation-no collection” policy is now also implemented  for
   barangay level on May 16, 2017. Several meetings with barangay  officials
   and focal persons of business companies  have  also  been  undertaken  to
   enforce the program  especially  on  the  establishment  of  MRF  in  the
   barangays.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that:


        a. The City Mayor require the  members  of  the  LDRRM  Council  to
           prepare a revised AIP to include  proposed  program,  activities
           and projects to be charged against the  unexpended  balances  of
           the LDRRMF.


        b. The City Accountant ensure that  expenses  charged  against  the
           unexpended balances of the LDRRMF are included  in  the  revised
           AIP pursuant to Item 6.5 of JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.


        c. Moreover, he must require  the  CSWD  to  submit  Memorandum  of
           Agreement (MOA) between the City and the five  beneficiaries  of
           carabaos and include in the MOA  the  responsibilities  of  both
           parties.


        d. Lastly, the City Mayor create monitoring team who will  evaluate
           the projects implemented charged against the LDRRMF.

   Management Comment:

        Recommendation is well taken.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that:


        a. The City Mayor require the  members  of  the  LDRRM  Council  to
           prepare a revised AIP to include  proposed  program,  activities
           and projects to be charged against the  unexpended  balances  of
           the LDRRMF.


        b. The City Accountant ensure that  expenses  charged  against  the
           unexpended balances of the LDRRMF are included  in  the  revised
           AIP pursuant to Item 6.5 of JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.


        c. Moreover, he must require  the  CSWD  to  submit  Memorandum  of
           Agreement (MOA) between the City and the five  beneficiaries  of
           carabaos and include in the MOA  the  responsibilities  of  both
           parties.


        d. Lastly, the City Mayor create monitoring team who will  evaluate
           the projects implemented charged against the LDRRMF.

   Management Comment:

        Recommendation is well taken.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Accountant strictly  comply  with  the
   documentary  requirements  on  the  implementation  and  liquidation   of
   financial assistance granted to NGOs/POs as provided by Section  2.2  COA
   Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

        Further,   the   City   Accountant   ascertain   that    no    fund
   transfer/financial assistance to NGO/PO shall be allowed if the  previous
   assistance has not yet been liquidated.


   Management Comment:


        Recommendation is well taken.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that City require the concerned barangays and  their
   officials to liquidate all financial assistance granted to them  pursuant
   to Item 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Moreover,
   the City refrain from granting additional assistance to  those  barangays
   which failed to liquidate the previous funds received.


   Management Comment:


        The  Accounting  Office  is  presently   monitoring   closely   the
   liquidation of financial assistance granted to barangays. In fact,  eight
   barangays have already liquidated the assistance granted them this  month
   of June 2017.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the ICO-City Treasurer and the City  Accountant
   reconcile their records at least quarterly as provided in Section  181(c)
   of GAAM, Volume I so any adjustments, if there are any, will be  effected
   immediately. As regards the discrepancy on the Cash in  Treasury  account
   under SEF, the two offices must conduct immediate  investigation  on  the
   noted discrepancy and prepare appropriate adjustments promptly to reflect
   the correct and reliable Cash in Treasury balance in all funds.

   Management Comment:


        The difference was reconciled on Feb.  28,  2017  per  JEV-2017-02-
   000219.

Recommendation:

        We  recommended  that  the  City  Mayor   require   the   concerned
   officials/personnel to submit the list of all on-going projects and those
   government PPAs to be implemented during the year and likewise inform the
   audit team of the required posting of project  signboards  and/or  public
   notices within the time specified per COA Circular No. 2013-004  so  that
   timely validation on completed and ongoing projects could be conducted.

   Management Comment:

        Recommendation is well taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that appropriation for Livelihood Program partake the nature of investment and capital expenditures in accordance with Section 2.3.2 of DILG-DBM Joint Cir. No. 01, s. 2005, in order to attain the desirable socio-economic development outcomes or goals by taking into consideration the following:

Appropriation Ordinance for the Livelihood Program shall stipulate that the program shall be a capital investment by classifying the budget under Capital Outlay;
Interest income shall be recognized immediately upon payment of the amortization; and
A separate book or special account shall be maintained by the Office of the City Accountant for this program, including the preparation of a separate financial statement, when necessary.

The Office of the City Livelihood and Cooperative shall also keep a record of the whole operation of the program to have complete information on its status.  It shall also coordinate with the Office of the City Accountant and City Treasurer to properly administer and monitor the program.
